candidates.direction_name: Trim PTX 64x64 Microkernel Register Footprint Back To 3-CTA Class
ranking_notes: This is the single best upside/risk tradeoff: the dominant bottleneck class (occupancy_latency_hiding_issue, severity 43.22) is directly caused by the 34-register delta produced last round.
If registers fall back to the 168 class, occupancy_limit_registers returns to 3 and we regain most of the latency hiding lost this round, while keeping the barrier stall wins of round 10.
Maps onto the human guidance family Register Reuse (Right-Left-Right-Left warp tile) almost literally, so the direction is also audit-aligned with state/human_guidance.md.
candidates.hypothesis: The round-10 wait-group collapse restored the PTX anchor and fixed the barrier and short_scoreboard stalls, but it caused registers/thread to jump 168 -> 202 because the restored microkernel keeps 16 f32 accumulator fragments plus live A/B fragments across both 64x64 row pairs. By reordering ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pair_64x64_ptx_microkernel so the two row pairs are interleaved column-by-column (Right-Left-Right-Left across RowPairBase = 0 and 2) instead of sequentially, and by reducing the simultaneous live PtxWmmaBf16Fragment count, we should be able to hold registers/thread below ~180 and restore launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 3. That directly attacks the dominant occupancy_latency_hiding_issue (severity 43.22) without touching barrier wins.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register-pressure-bound occupancy: 202 regs/thread caps SM residency at 2 CTAs, starving the long_scoreboard / mio_throttle latency hiding budget at 16.58% active warps.
candidates.risk: Moderate. The microkernel ordering is performance-sensitive but fully preserves the WMMA math; correctness risk is low and register pressure can be verified with ncu launch__registers_per_thread on the first compile.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if launch__registers_per_thread does not fall below 190 after the interleave rewrite, or if the rewrite regresses barrier above 8% or runtime above 25.5 ms.
expected_local_changes: Rework ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pair_64x64_ptx_microkernel so the two row pairs are interleaved column-by-column instead of both sequentially materialized, shrinking the live A-fragment window.
Confirm nvcc does not inflate fragment state by adding --maxrregcount guard only if necessary, avoiding spills to local memory.
Leave wait_group_0 / wait_group_1 ordering from round 10 untouched so recovered barrier and short_scoreboard gains are preserved.

candidates.direction_name: Deepen PTX 128x128 Async Copy Pipeline To A 3-Stage Pg2s Buffer
ranking_notes: Directly targets the two regressed stall classes (long_scoreboard, mio_throttle) without relying on occupancy recovery.
Maps onto the Pg2s, Async Copy, and Stage families from human guidance in one edit.
Ranked second because it grows shared memory and is more fragile than the register trim in dir_01; also, if dir_01 succeeds, much of the latency hiding gap closes for free.
candidates.hypothesis: Even if we cannot drop registers immediately, the PTX anchor now pays 5.53% long_scoreboard and 4.0% mio_throttle (both regressed this round). That is a classic symptom of under-hidden global -> shared latency at low occupancy. Moving the double-buffer (2-stage) cp.async scheme to a 3-stage Pg2s ring (so one MMA issue cycle, one cp.async wait_group_2, and one in-flight refill overlap) should cut long_scoreboard and mio_throttle without requiring more active warps, since the hiding now comes from pipeline depth rather than warp count.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Global-to-shared latency hiding at low occupancy: long_scoreboard 5.53 (+3.26 vs prev) and mio_throttle 4.0 (+2.23 vs prev) indicate warps are stalling on cp.async completion instead of making tensor-pipe progress.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:119-132 (FixedHotBandTile128x128: kASharedBytes uses factor 4, which can accommodate 3 stages)
candidates.risk: Moderate-high. A 3-stage ring touches the prolog, the steady-state refill order, and the epilogue drain simultaneously; requires careful wait_group_2 / wait_group_1 / wait_group_0 sequencing and 50% more shared memory for A/B buffers.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if long_scoreboard does not drop below 4% or if shared memory allocation pushes occupancy_limit_shared_mem below the current occupancy_limit_registers class.
expected_local_changes: Grow the a_shared / b_shared arrays from [2] to [3] stages (already allowed by the factor-4 budget in FixedHotBandTile128x128).
Replace the prolog two cp.async_commit_group calls + wait_group_1 with a three-commit prolog + wait_group_2 schedule.
In the steady-state loop, keep the cp.async_wait_group_0 / __syncthreads() sequence but fire refills two tiles ahead of current stage so three commits are always in flight.

ranking_notes: Kept alive because it is the highest-ceiling branch in the repo and directly maps to the persistent human Tiling and Register Reuse guidance.
Ranked third because prior rounds already measured two negative outcomes on reopen attempts, and the current loop has cheaper, more targeted options (dir_01, dir_02) on the nearer family.
Should only be exploited after dir_01 either succeeds and plateaus or fails cleanly on register pressure.
candidates.hypothesis: The persistent human guidance explicitly calls for 256x128 block tiling with 64x64 warp tiling and a Right-Left-Right-Left warp-tile traversal. FixedHotBandTile256x128 already exists in the repo (kWarpMmaTilesM = 4, kWarpMmaTilesN = 4, 8 warps/CTA) and previous rounds closed the pivot family due to correctness/regression cost, but the current state (occupancy pinned at 2 CTAs/SM anyway) makes a larger tile attractive because it amortizes the A-panel load and the per-warp register accumulator over 4x the math. This is the highest-ceiling branch if dir_01 and dir_02 fail to close the 6.7 ms remaining gap to the 18 ms target.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Tile-granularity / register-reuse ceiling: at 128x128 the A panel is reloaded for every 128 columns; a 256x128 panel doubles A reuse per cp.async batch while keeping B staging compact.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader structural rewrite. Two prior rounds closed 256x128 pivot attempts on correctness/regression grounds; reopening requires re-verifying writer-ownership and half-panel export mapping, and the correctness-first reopens historically regressed to the 28-30 ms class.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
median runtime versus the 24.195072 ms accepted base
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately on any correctness failure or any measurement above the 28 ms class; do not spend more than one round on this family without a new machine-state change.
expected_local_changes: Restore the correctness-safe 256x128 writer ownership path.
Wire the PTX anchor launch to the 256x128 kernel for the pivot hot band while keeping the 64x96 tail and 64x384 row-band paths unchanged.
Apply the Right-Left-Right-Left warp tile traversal on the 4x4 = 16 accumulator tiles so B-fragment reuse increases across the warp tile.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
